releases.notes: fix(ci): use GITHUB_TOKEN for gh release view, PUBLISH_TOKEN only for dispatch

feat(stops): refactor stopName to stopKey with mode-aware departures
Rename StopData.stopName to stopKey throughout all layers. Stop keys
encode transport mode (e.g. "Ohrada|TRAM") enabling per-mode favorites,
hidden stops, and filtered departure boards.
- Departure board splits into mode cards when grouping is off
- Search results show mode-split cards based on setting
- Favorites stored separately per grouping mode (swap on toggle)
- Fix navigation tab stuck state when pendingSearchRoute persists
[release][publish]
feat(nearby): split stops by transport mode setting (#60)
"Group stops by name" toggle in Settings (default: on). When off,
stops with multiple transport types get separate cards with suffix
(e.g., "Olovnice — Bus", "Olovnice — Train"). Favorites/hidden
check both display name and base name for compatibility.
feat(annotation): include user name from report settings
feat(update): show "What's new" dialog on version update [release][publish]
Messages tied to specific versions in UpdateMessages.kt. On first
launch after update, shows dialog with all messages from versions
between last seen and current. Gate GitHub Release on [release] tag,
separate from [publish] for WhatsApp notifications.
feat(tools): add /annotation skill and evaluation framework
- /annotation skill: fetch new annotations, save locally, close issues, evaluate
- evaluate.py: replay all saved annotations against current and old model params
- Save annotation issues #74, #75, #76 as local JSON files

feat(trip): tune position model from annotation data [publish]
Parameters tuned against 67 annotations from 2 real tram rides:
- TRANSITION_MS: 15s → 20s (longer departing/arriving phases)
- MAX_ALPHA: 1.0 → 0.85 (never fully snap to API)
- MIN_API_AGE_MS: 0 → 10s (smooth API corrections)
- Add app version to annotation data
- Save tuning results and replay script in model-tuning/
Backward jumps reduced 60-70%, at-stop accuracy +5%, no backward guard
(tested — hurts accuracy more than it helps).

fix(trip): correct phase layout and stale state in position model [publish]
- Reorder phases: AT_STOP is now last in segment (vehicle dwells
  before departure), DEPARTING is first (vehicle just left)
- Fix stale captured values in tick loop with rememberUpdatedState
- No green blinking when vehicle is not tracked (schedule-only mode)
- Annotation upload on bar dismiss (trigger: manual) + 30min auto-stale
- Annotation issue body includes trigger type

feat(trip): minimized bar after dismissing route view [publish]
Dismissing the trip detail sheet shows a compact bar at the bottom
with route number and headsign. Tap to reopen (fetches fresh data),
swipe to dismiss completely.
feat(dev): trip annotation buttons for position model tuning
Arrive/Depart buttons on each stop row (±5 from active) in developer
mode. Saves timestamp, stop context, API state + age, and model
prediction per tap. Auto-posts as GitHub issue (label: annotation)
when session is idle for 1 hour.
feat(trip): time-fusion position model, developer mode, GPS fix
- Replace spring-velocity estimator with stateless time-fusion model:
  5 discrete states per segment (AT_STOP, DEPARTING, TRANSIT, ARRIVING),
  blends API snapshot with schedule using confidence decay
- Add developer mode toggle in Settings: gates Report Issue, My Reports,
  and logcat debug output in trip detail
- Fix GPS cold start: try cached location (getLastLocation) before
  requesting fresh fix, reuse if younger than refresh interval (#72)
- Rename CI notification trigger from [release] to [publish]

refactor(ci): route WhatsApp notify through release-publisher
Replace direct Windmill call with a repository_dispatch to the public
release-publisher repo. Recipients are encrypted with AES-256-GCM using
the shared NOTIFY_KEY before inclusion in the dispatch payload.
feat(ui): pull-to-refresh on version history and my reports
Also show error message when version list fails to load, and fix
unnecessary non-null assertions in MyReportsScreen.

perf: ship 512² fronts + 256² cover, drop canvas DPR cap [release] [publish]
Two changes that compound:
- scanner/build.py now downsizes data/ → scanner/art/ via Pillow
  Lanczos. Originals at 1024² stay in data/ for print PDFs (printed
  card quality unaffected). scanner/art/ shrinks 46MB → 9.6MB.
  Card fronts: 512², ~530KB each. Cover: 256², ~107KB.
- scanner/animations/plane.js: DPR cap was already 1, kept that way.
  Each textured-tile draw now samples a 256² source instead of 1024²,
  a 16× cheaper texture sample, and the canvas backing buffer is
  4× smaller than at native DPR.
Net: plane animation should run smoothly during the 5s glide on
mid-range phones. APK shrinks correspondingly.
feat(scanner): home icon on scan screen too — back to Start [release] [publish]
0.1.5 only had the home icon inside playback mode, so after tapping Start
there was no way to cancel and return to the Start screen. Add the same
icon to the scan screen; clicking it stops MindAR and restores the Start
screen. Hidden during playback (exit-playback icon takes over there).

feat(scanner): home icon on scan screen too — back to Start [release] [publish]

feat(app): in-app APK download+install, home icon, hide version label [release] [publish]
Three UX fixes in one go:
1. Native ApkUpdaterPlugin — JS calls
   window.Capacitor.Plugins.ApkUpdater.downloadAndInstall({ url }), which
   streams the APK into the app's cacheDir and fires ACTION_VIEW via
   FileProvider, same pattern as prague-mhd-dashboard's ApkInstaller.kt.
   User still taps "Install" on the system dialog (Android requirement
   for non-Play-Store updates). No more leaving the app to a browser.
2. Top-left home/X icon on the playback screen so the user can abandon
   the current song and return to scan mode (matches the scan-new
   button but more discoverable in the top-left corner).
3. Version label hidden by default. It was showing on every load for
   no reason. Now only surfaces when something errors (yt, audio
   resolve, audio play, glide, update). A successful play clears it.

fix(app): detect native via isNativePlatform, stat cache, surface real error [release] [publish]
The 0.1.3 APK reported \"audio play blocked: Not supported\". Three
changes to diagnose and likely fix:
- Use \`window.Capacitor.isNativePlatform()\` to decide web-vs-APK
  instead of probing same-origin audio with HEAD. The probe was
  flaky inside the WebView's asset scheme.
- Call \`Filesystem.stat\` before \`getUri\` so a genuine cache miss
  actually falls through to download instead of silently handing
  \`<audio>\` a stale/empty URI.
- Swap the single-line catch for a staged error: the version label
  now shows "resolve err:…" vs "play NotSupportedError:…" so we
  know which phase broke.

fix(app): detect native via isNativePlatform, stat cache, surface real error [release] [publish]
The 0.1.3 APK reported \"audio play blocked: Not supported\". Three
changes to diagnose and likely fix:
- Use \`window.Capacitor.isNativePlatform()\` to decide web-vs-APK
  instead of probing same-origin audio with HEAD. The probe was
  flaky inside the WebView's asset scheme.
- Call \`Filesystem.stat\` before \`getUri\` so a genuine cache miss
  actually falls through to download instead of silently handing
  \`<audio>\` a stale/empty URI.
- Swap the single-line catch for a staged error: the version label
  now shows "resolve err:…" vs "play NotSupportedError:…" so we
  know which phase broke.
feat(app): lazy-load mp3s, shrink APK from 205MB to ~90MB [release] [publish]
The APK previously bundled every mp3 (~112MB). Now it ships without
them and downloads each track on first scan, caching in the app's
private Data/audio/ dir via @capacitor/filesystem so subsequent scans
play instantly.
- scanner/build.py: emit absolute audio_url for each card so the APK
  has a source to fetch from.
- scanner/index.html: replace direct playLocalAudio(src, startSeconds)
  with resolveAudioSrc(card) — tries same-origin path first (web
  deploy works unchanged), then Capacitor Filesystem cache, then
  HTTP fetch + base64 writeFile for the next boot.
- CI workflow: `mv scanner/audio /tmp/_scanner_audio` before cap sync
  so those files stay on Cloudflare Pages but don't ship in the APK.
- Small "Loading song…" pill while the first-scan fetch is in flight.
Security note: the plan deliberately defers URL obfuscation/gating.
The audio files are already enumerable via cards.json today; real
protection would need a signed-URL origin or Cloudflare Worker gate,
which is disproportionate for the current scale.

feat(app): in-APK update banner — polls release-publisher for newer APK
APKs are static snapshots of a specific commit. When a newer APK is
published on release-publisher, the running app polls
docs/releases.json, compares its own versionName to the latest, and
shows a yellow "Update available: vX.Y.Z" banner with a Download
button that opens the .apk URL — Android's package installer picks it
up from there. Dismissal is remembered for 24h in localStorage.
Implementation:
- CI stamps scanner/apk-version.json before cap sync so the APK knows
  its own version without needing any Capacitor plugin.
- Web deploy has no apk-version.json, so the banner stays dormant for
  browser visitors.
- Version label now shows "v0.1.2 (web 0.0.NN)" inside the APK to make
  the two-version story obvious. Plain web shows just "v0.0.NN".
- REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES permission added so ACTION_VIEW on the APK
  URL triggers the system installer.
Bumps to v0.1.2 to exercise the pipeline. [release] [publish]

ci: pass web_url in dispatch payload, bump to v0.1.1 [release] [publish]
release-publisher now accepts a web_url field and renders an
"Open Web App" link. Wiring pexesongy's dispatch to send
https://pexesongy.pages.dev/ so the card links to the live scanner.
Patch bump only — no scanner code changed.

Capacitor APK of the pexesongy scanner. All 18 cards with local mp3
playback, rays + plane animations, works offline after first launch.
Distributed via release-publisher.
ci: commit package-lock.json so `npm ci` works on runners
npm ci requires the lockfile; I had it gitignored from the first
Capacitor commit, so the workflow failed on first build. Including
it also means reproducible npm installs, which is the whole point.
ci: fix missing Android SDK + wipe keystore + build AAB too
Review pass on the release workflow:
- Add android-actions/setup-android@v3 step: gradle has no SDK without
  it on Ubuntu runners; previous version would fail on first run.
- Decode keystore with explicit \$GITHUB_WORKSPACE path (matches the
  KEYSTORE_FILE env var passed to gradle).
- Remove keystore.jks after build regardless of success, so any later
  step running on the same job can't read it.
- Build bundleRelease alongside assembleRelease and include the .aab in
  both artifact upload and GitHub release, so Play Store publishing
  path is there later without workflow changes.
Matches the structure of prague-mhd-dashboard's build.yml.
ci: sign release APK with user-provided keystore
- app/android/app/build.gradle: release signingConfig reads
  KEYSTORE_FILE/KEYSTORE_PASSWORD/KEY_ALIAS/KEY_PASSWORD env vars; inert
  block falls back to debug signing when the env var is not set.
- release.yml: decode KEYSTORE_BASE64 secret to keystore.jks, pass all
  four credentials to gradle, build assembleRelease (not assembleDebug),
  pick the release APK path.
- .gitignore: *.jks, *.keystore, keystore.b64.
Secrets configured on jirip/pexesongy:
  KEYSTORE_BASE64, KEYSTORE_PASSWORD, KEY_ALIAS, KEY_PASSWORD
  (+ existing PUBLISH_TOKEN, NOTIFY_KEY).
ci: match prague-mhd-dashboard pattern — [release]/[publish] commit markers
Rewritten release workflow: builds APK on every push, only creates a
GitHub release when commit subject contains [release], only dispatches
to release-publisher when subject contains [publish]. versionName
pulled from app/android/app/build.gradle.
Same notify.txt + NOTIFY_KEY encryption flow as pdf2jpg and
prague-mhd-dashboard.
